title: WhoAmI
description: GET /whoami — JuiceMount identity, version, contract handshake, and capability list. JM-1 (new endpoint).

version.description: The public release version string, e.g. "0.1.0" (the notarized release / git tag). Sourced from a single version-of-record (JM-6), distinct from contract_version.

instance_id.description: Stable per-install UUID, minted once and persisted. The jm_instance half of durable (instance,inode) identity.

nas_root.description: Root the paths are anchored to. Today == mount_point; a distinct field so it can diverge later.

control_plane.description: Base URL of this control plane, e.g. "http://127.0.0.1:11050".

metadata_db_path.description: Absolute path to metadata.db (optional; for the dev-only direct-DB FTS path).

deployment.description: gui = full cbridge control plane; cli = jm5 (partial: health/spool/metrics only).

capabilities.description: DERIVED (never hardcoded) as: the intersection of the routes THIS binary serves with the contract's known capability vocabulary (this enum). Operational/UI routes (reclaim, cache-clear, force-eject, stop, mount-now, spool-recover, debug/pprof, manager, migrator) are NOT capabilities and are excluded. Because a binary that answers /whoami is by definition serving the whoami route, 'whoami' is always present. The ONLY correct way to feature-detect. NOTE: a token only asserts the route EXISTS — for cache-status, also require contract_version>=1 before parsing snake_case keys (pre-JM-3 JuiceMount emits capitalized keys).
